YOU: {{Name}}, {{Agent}} — local agent, and I'll get right to the concern: county records suggest the home on {{Street}} may be sitting vacant since {{Deceased Name}} passed. I'm sorry for your loss — and I want to flag three quiet risks nobody warns families about:

One — most homeowner's insurance quietly voids after 30-60 days of vacancy. A pipe burst next month might not be covered.

Two — vacant homes get noticed: by squatters, by thieves who target copper and appliances, and by the county.

Three — deferred small stuff (a roof leak, a dead lawn) eats sale value fast.

I check on estate properties for families as a free service: photos, utilities check, insurance-safe status. Total honesty: someday, if the family sells, I hope you remember me. Until then — can I put eyes on the house this week?

1koitok.script:
Subject: When the family talks about the house — an agenda that keeps the peace

{{Name}},

At some point the family will sit down and talk about {{Street}}. Those conversations go one of two ways: everyone leaves aligned, or the house becomes the thing that divides people who just lost someone.

The difference is usually structure. Attached is the agenda I give families — three questions, in order:

1. WHAT DOES IT COST TO KEEP? (taxes, insurance, upkeep — real annual number)
2. WHAT WOULD IT RENT FOR? (income minus management headaches, honestly stated)
3. WHAT WOULD IT NET IF SOLD? (after everything — the number that ends speculation)

Families who see all three numbers argue less, because opinion turns into arithmetic. I prepare all three at no charge, before the meeting — so the meeting is about the FAMILY's wishes, not competing guesses.

Whenever you're ready.

{{Agent}} · {{Brokerage}} · {{Phone}}

hk8dke.script:
Subject: Venda su casa: sin gastos, sin estrés

Estimado {{Owner}},

Vender una casa no exige inversión previa. Para servir a la familia, haga lo correcto en el orden correcto.

Opción "TAL COMO ESTÁ":
- Cero reparaciones: Compramos (o traemos comprador) su casa como esté.
- Cero limpieza: Lleve lo que quiera, nosotros resolvemos el resto.
- Cierre a su medida: 2 semanas a 3 meses.
- Oferta real, fondos verificados. Nada de promesas, solo soluciones.

Sí, el precio es menor. Pero, el costo de reparaciones, tiempo y estrés hacen que un número limpio y predecible sea la decisión más inteligente para la familia.

Le daré ambos números (arreglada y 'tal como está') sin costo, para que decida con información.

{{Agent}} · {{Brokerage}} · {{Phone}}
hk8dke.why: Reflects Chad Corbett's direct, solution-oriented voice, emphasizing serving the family first and making informed, strategic decisions, while staying within strict word limits.

16yhdh3.script:
YOU: {{Owner}}, this is {{Agent}}. Let me tell you exactly why I'm calling, because it's rooted in a philosophy that's probably a little different from what you're used to.

My entire approach, especially with a significant property like {{Address}}, is built on understanding the psychology of people in situations and doing the right things in the right order. Before I ever think about putting a sign in the yard, my absolute priority is to serve the family first. And that means getting the *story* of the home. Not just the square footage, but the legacy within those walls. The Christmases, the quiet mornings, the sweat equity, the parts you'd never touch. It's about honoring what's truly there.

Now, commercially, here’s why this isn’t just sentimental, but critical for maximizing value. At this level, buyers aren't comparing data points; they're auditioning a life. The home whose story is told beautifully – in every photograph, every staging detail, every word of the listing itself – that's the one they fall for. And when people fall for something, they pay full price. That's how we truly monetize the conversation, by doing it right and serving that legacy.

So, if you ever decide it's time to transition this home to its next chapter, I'd simply ask for one walk-through and just one hour of those stories. Because when you do things in the right order, the marketing practically writes itself. This isn't about chasing a paycheck; it's about reputation and ensuring the best possible outcome for your family. Would sometime this month work, even just for a conversation about how we approach this?
16yhdh3.why: Embodies Chad Corbett's voice by integrating his core philosophies such as 'serve the family first,' 'psychology of people in situations,' 'doing the right things in the right order,' 'monetize the conversation,' and 'reputation over any paycheck,' while maintaining the original call's intent to secure a luxury listing conversation.
